content.problemStatement:
Chef's fans want to celebrate the special occasion of his birthday, so they are writing letters to him. Chef has received NNN such letters from NNN fans (numbered 111 through NNN). Initially, for each valid iii, he assigned a beauty BiB_iBi​ to the letter from the iii-th fan (these values may even be negative, for letters that are not written well).

Now, Chef wants you to process QQQ queries on these letters. There are two types of queries:


`Q x y`: Chef wants to choose integers lll and rrr such that 1≤l≤x≤y≤r≤N1 \le l \le x \le y \le r \le N1≤l≤x≤y≤r≤N and read all the letters from fans l,l+1,…,rl, l+1, \ldots, rl,l+1,…,r. Find the maximum possible value of the sum of beauties of the letters he reads.
`U x v`: Chef reevaluates the beauty of the letter from the xxx-th fan. The new value of BxB_xBx​ becomes vvv.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.
Each of the next QQQ lines describes a query in the format described above.
content.outputFormat: For each query of the first type, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum sum of beauties.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: For the first query, Chef should read the letters from fans 222, 333, 444 and 555.
